reasoning: The informal statement involves two independent natural numbers: an index m for consecutive cubes (m³ and (m+1)³) and n for the square root of their difference, e.g., (8)³−(7)³=169=13² gives n=13 and 2n−1=25=5². The Lean formalization conflates these into a single variable n, writing (n+1)³ − n³ = n². This is a special case (setting m=n in the informal claim), so Lean is implied by the informal statement but not conversely. Moreover, (n+1)³ − n³ = 3n² + 3n + 1 = n² requires 2n² + 3n + 1 = 0, which has no natural-number solutions, so the Lean hypothesis is never satisfiable and the Lean statement is vacuously true — trivially provable while not capturing the real mathematical content.
